chunks.text: Sergey Levine is an Associate Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences at UC Berkeley. He received a BS and MS in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2009, and a Ph.D. in Computer Science from Stanford University in 2014. He joined UC Berkeley's faculty in fall 2016. His research focuses on machine learning for decision making and control, with emphasis on deep learning and reinforcement learning algorithms.
chunks.question: What are Sergey Levine's academic background and research focus?
chunks.answer: Stanford degrees in CS, focuses on ML for decision making.

chunks.text: Sergey Levine's work applications include autonomous robots and vehicles, computer vision, and graphics. His research develops algorithms for end-to-end training of deep neural network policies combining perception and control, scalable inverse reinforcement learning algorithms, and deep reinforcement learning algorithms.
chunks.question: What applications does Sergey Levine's research have?
chunks.answer: Autonomous robots, vehicles, computer vision, and graphics.

chunks.text: Sergey Levine's research areas are Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics (CIR). He is affiliated with research centers including Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab (BAIR), Berkeley Deep Drive (BDD), and CITRIS People and Robots (CPAR).
chunks.question: Which research areas and centers is Sergey Levine associated with?
chunks.answer: AI, CIR, BAIR, BDD, and CPAR.

chunks.text: Sergey Levine teaches CS 185 and CS 285, both titled 'Deep Reinforcement Learning, Decision Making, and Control'. Classes are held on Wednesdays and Fridays at Hearst Field Annex A1.
chunks.question: What courses does Sergey Levine teach and where?
chunks.answer: CS 185 and CS 285 at Hearst Field Annex A1.

chunks.text: Selected honors and awards include NSF Presidential Early Career Award for Scientists & Engineers (PECASE) in 2024, Okawa Research Grant in 2021, Sloan Research Fellow in 2019, NSF Faculty Early Career Development Award (CAREER) in 2017, MIT Tech Review Top 35 Innovators Under 35 (TR35) in 2016, and Office of Naval Research Young Investigator in 2016.
chunks.question: What are some of Sergey Levine's notable awards?
chunks.answer: PECASE, Okawa Grant, Sloan Fellow, CAREER, TR35, ONR.
